text: Light takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to travel from the Sun to Earth, so you always see the Sun as it was minutes ago.
DNA in a single human cell would stretch about 2 meters if unwound, yet it fits inside a nucleus a few microns across.
Water is densest at about 4 degrees C, which is why ice floats and lakes freeze from the top down.
The human body has roughly the same number of bacterial cells as human cells, most of them in the gut.
Lightning heats the air it passes through to around 30,000 K - about five times hotter than the surface of the Sun.
A teaspoon of neutron-star material would weigh about a billion tons because its matter is packed to nuclear density.
Honey found in ancient Egyptian tombs was still edible after 3,000 years; its low water content and acidity stop microbes growing.
Trees move water from roots to leaves largely by transpiration pulling on cohesive water columns - no pump required.
The speed of sound in air (~343 m/s at 20 C) depends on temperature, not on how loud the sound is.
Helium was discovered in the Sun's spectrum in 1868, before it was found on Earth - its name comes from Helios, the Greek sun god.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue, copper-based blood that carries oxygen more efficiently in cold, low-oxygen water.
Earth's inner core is solid despite being hotter than the outer core, because the immense pressure raises iron's melting point.
